source.title: Hippocampal replay is not a simple function of experience
authors: Matthijs A A van der Meer

confidence_rationale: Neuron paper, single-unit recording in rat HPC during a two-sequence behavioural task with controlled experience frequency manipulation. Direct empirical evidence for compositional replay: forward AND backward replay of behavioural sequence B occurred when rat had been performing A for 10+ minutes (i.e. NOT current experience). Replay of nonlocal B was MORE frequent when B was infrequently experienced. Replay also constructed never-experienced novel-path sequences. This directly supports the user's architectural reading that hippocampal replay is compositional / generative, not just experience-replication. Confidence 0.85 reflects strong empirical methodology and direct mapping to MECH-285 / MECH-292 / MECH-293.
failure_signatures: The novel-path sequences observed are spatial trajectories within the same physical environment. Whether replay constructs novel sequences across abstract / type-level / option-level dimensions (i.e. whether the compositional capacity extends from spatial-paths to abstraction-graphs) is plausible but not directly demonstrated.

Replay frequency was inversely related to experience frequency for the *non-current* sequence -- but this is a counterintuitive result that complicates simple staleness-priority accounts. The MECH-285 priority-weighted sleep replay sampler currently uses staleness, not inverse-experience-frequency. The two are correlated but not identical, and getting this wrong could mismatch the substrate behavior.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Replay of behavioural sequences in hippocampus during sharp wave ripple complexes provides a potential mechanism for memory consolidation and learning of knowledge structures. On a task with two distinct behavioural sequences (A and B), forward and backward replay of B occurred even when rats had been performing A for >10 min. Replay of nonlocal B was MORE frequent when B was infrequently experienced. Neither forward nor backward sequences preferentially represented highly experienced trajectories. Construction of never-experienced novel-path sequences was also observed. Replay reflected all physically available trajectories within the environment, suggesting a role in active learning and maintenance of the cognitive map.
mapping.ree_translation: For MECH-285 (sleep replay sampler -- priority-weighted anchor sampling), MECH-292 (ranked ghost-goal bank), and MECH-293 (waking ghost-goal probes): biology supports the compositional-recombination reading of hippocampal replay. Replay is NOT a literal re-run of recent trajectories; it is a generative process that constructs sequences -- including never-experienced novel paths -- spanning the available cognitive map. This validates the existing MECH-292/293 architecture (probes seeded from anchors with goal-match cosine similarity, not from recency) and supports adding compositional sleep operators that recombine across goal contexts.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper's 'novel paths' are spatial. Whether compositional replay extends to abstract or hierarchical task structure is a separate question. Also: the inverse-experience-frequency finding for non-current B is counterintuitive and complicates staleness-priority models. MECH-285's priority weights might need refinement to reflect this signal. Direct transfer to type-level / option-level compositional replay (which the user's prediction implicitly requires) is plausible but not directly demonstrated.
mapping.source_context: Single-unit recording in rat dorsal CA1 during a behavioural paradigm with two distinct learned sequences (A and B). SWR replay events identified by ripple-band power; sequence direction (forward vs backward vs novel-path) classified by Bayesian decoding of place-cell ensemble activity.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (Neuron, careful experimental design with controlled experience-frequency manipulation, replicated framework). Mapping fidelity high: the compositional / generative reading of replay maps directly onto MECH-285/292/293 architecture and validates the existing priority-weighted (rather than experience-replicating) approach. Transfer risk modest because the compositional-replay-spans-cognitive-map result transfers naturally to whatever cognitive map REE builds, including the type / option extensions in V4.
